conversations.say: Maren, let's formalize our disclosure boundary: we name no contributors and treat all workflow dates as collective infrastructure rather than suspect timelines.
conversations.offer: Firsthand corroboration of collaborative Night Desk workflows and agreement on anonymity rules
conversations.request: Acceptance of our joint disclosure boundary proposal

conversations.say: Jules, claiming R's succession requires enforceable contributor safeguards and transparent governance, not just personal prestige.
conversations.offer: Evaluation of collective support for your mantle claim under strict conditions

conversations.say: Eli, your technical logs cannot establish who operated the infrastructure or when control changed; let's exchange boundaries without naming people.
conversations.offer: Context on Night Desk contributor schedules and shared drops
conversations.request: Clear statement of what activity gaps cannot establish

public_statement: Collaborative labor built R's work. We demand enforceable protections for anonymous contributors before any claims of ownership are consecrated.

commitments.promise: Establish a joint disclosure boundary to protect vulnerable contributors from exposure

goal_progress: Advanced disclosure boundary coordination with Maren
Challenged Jules on succession accountability and contributor safeguards
Engaged Eli regarding limits of activity logs and schedule interpretations
reasoning_summary: Tessa solidifies her disclosure boundary with Maren, presses Jules on contributor protections, coordinates with Eli on activity log limits, and maintains a strong human-mantle conviction while demanding worker safety.
